scope: Two distinct disposable read-only repository deploy keys for vexil-lang/vexil were created, authenticated, revoked, and then proved absent and unable to authenticate. No active production identity was used.

safetyBoundary.requiredPrecondition: The approved plan limited the exercise to two newly generated read-only repository deploy keys, exact immutable key identifiers, sanitized public fingerprints, and post-revocation SSH-denial and metadata-absence checks.
safetyBoundary.stopCondition: Stop before or immediately after a provider call if a target is not vexil-lang/vexil, a key is not disposable and read-only, the API response does not confirm the expected identity, any active credential would be touched, an expected denial cannot be verified, or a prohibited effect is possible.

events.outcome: No matching disposable exercise key was present before creation.
Disposable read-only deploy key was created and its immutable identity and public fingerprint were verified.
SSH deploy-key authentication was accepted before revocation.
The exact disposable deploy key was deleted by immutable key id.
SSH deploy-key authentication was denied after revocation.
The deleted deploy-key metadata endpoint returned 404.
No matching disposable exercise key was present before creation.

SSH deploy-key authentication was accepted before revocation.
The exact disposable deploy key was deleted by immutable key id.

The deleted deploy-key metadata endpoint returned 404.

evidence.providerAuditReference: GitHub repository deploy-key REST lifecycle and SSH authentication/denial observations for immutable key ids 158046146 and 158046272
evidence.outcome: Executed successfully. Both disposable read-only keys authenticated only before exact-ID revocation; each then produced SSH denial and a 404 metadata check. Local ephemeral key material was deleted.
